description: In early 2021, WhatsApp announced a controversial update to its privacy policy, leading to a massive spike in downloads for the encrypted alternative Signal. This was a critical test of whether privacy-centric tech could outcompete convenience-centric tech.

key_events.description: WhatsApp privacy policy update notification is released.
Elon Musk tweets 'Use Signal', causing a surge in downloads.
WhatsApp delays implementation of policy, citing misinformation.
General users drift back to WhatsApp due to network requirements.

calibration_notes: Illustrates how a temporary 'privacy protest' can fail when it disrupts the core network effect.
